subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the theme of universal brotherhood and equality
Examine the poet’s message about war, peace, and human relationships
Identify and interpret poetic devices like repetition, rhyme, and imagery
Develop critical thinking through reflection and discussion
Connect the poem’s moral lessons to real-life situations

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Background of the poet and poem
Theme of human equality and peace
Poem as a social and moral commentary
teaching_strategy: Teacher reads the poem aloud with proper intonation
Discuss the poet’s purpose and context
student_activities: Listen attentively and note difficult words
Discuss meaning of unfamiliar terms in pairs

lesson_plan.title: Theme and Central Idea
topics_covered: War, peace, and consequences of hatred
teaching_strategy: Break the poem stanza by stanza to highlight main ideas
Discuss examples from students’ lives and history
student_activities: Write main idea of each stanza in own words
Share real-life examples of unity or conflict

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Devices and Style
teaching_strategy: Point out repeated lines and rhymes
Discuss how imagery conveys moral and emotion
student_activities: Identify poetic devices in the poem
Explain their effect in conveying the message

lesson_plan.title: Character and Perspective Analysis
topics_covered: Use of collective pronouns and inclusive language
teaching_strategy: Discuss poet’s use of language to appeal to all humans
student_activities: Underline words and phrases indicating shared humanity
Explain in own words how poet unites readers

lesson_plan.title: Moral, Reflection, and Application
topics_covered: Lesson on equality, peace, and empathy
Connecting poem’s message to modern life
teaching_strategy: Guide students to reflect on ethical and social implications
Encourage discussion on application in daily life
student_activities: Write a paragraph on the moral lesson learned
Share real-life examples demonstrating brotherhood and understanding

teaching_aids: Handouts with stanza-wise explanation and poetic devices
teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyze the poet’s perspective, recognize universal human values, and apply lessons of empathy and equality in their own lives.
